subject: Computer Networking
title: Single Sign-On
description: A short lesson to help you apply Single Sign-On.

hook.text: One login, access to everything: email, CRM, HR, cloud. SSO makes that seamless and secure.

buildup.text: SSO uses a central identity provider. When you log in once, it issues tokens for every connected application.

discovery.text: SAML and OIDC carry the identity assertion. The app trusts the identity provider instead of managing passwords.

twist.text: If the identity provider is breached, every connected app is exposed. SSO is a single point of convenience — and risk.

climax.text: That's why MFA on the identity provider is critical. Protecting that one login protects everything behind it.

punchline.text: SSO means one strong login replaces many weak ones.

quiz.question: What is the main risk of Single Sign-On?
options: A breach of the identity provider exposes all connected apps
